provisions.title: Public Holidays
provisions.content: [The word “Holidays” in this heading should not be capitalised.] (1) The days specified in the Schedule shall be public holidays. (2) When a public holiday falls on a Sunday the following Monday shall also be a public holiday, unless that Monday is a public holiday. (3) The President may by proclamation in the Gazette declare any day in a particular year, not being a public holiday in terms of subsection (1) or (2), to be a public holiday.

provisions.title: Interpretation of laws
provisions.content: Any reference in any law to a public holiday shall be construed as a reference to a public holiday referred to in section 1.

provisions.title: Repeal of Act 7 of 1987
provisions.content: The Public Holidays Act, 1987, is hereby repealed.

provisions.title: Short title and commencement
provisions.content: This Act shall be called the Public Holidays Act, 1990, and shall come into operation on 1 February 1991. Schedule New Year’s Day Good Friday Easter Monday Independence Day (the twenty-first day of March) Workers Day (the first day of May) Cassinga Day (the fourth day of May) Ascension Day Africa Day (the twenty-fifth day of May) Heroes Day (the twenty-sixth day of August) Day of the Namibian Women and International Human Rights Day (the tenth day of December) [The name of this public holiday is substituted by Act 16 of 2004 .] Christmas Day (the twenty-fifth day of December) Family Day (the twenty-sixth day of December). History of this document 17 December 2004 this version Amended by Public Holidays Amendment Act, 2004 01 February 1991 Commenced 20 December 1990 Published in Government Gazette 125 06 December 1990 Assented to Related documents Is amended by Public Holidays Amendment Act, 2004 [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About NamibLII NamibLII is a project of the Law Reform and Development Commission managed by the Directorate: Law Reform of the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Namibia. In terms of the Law Reform and Development Commission Act, 1991 (Act No. 29 of 1991), the Commission is mandated to consolidate or codify any branch of the law or to introduce other measures aimed at making the law more readily accessible. NamibLII is the embodiment of this mandate making annotated statutes, judgments, and gazettes available in a user-friendly format.
